overview: This quadrants Pauper deck leverages food counters and artifact recursion to assemble a high-value combo engine. By cycling Myr Retriever and Cauldron Familiar, players manipulate counters from Lembas to generate exponential life gain or unblockable board presence. Success depends on protecting the core pieces until the loop resolves.
win_conditions: Generating infinite life through food counter manipulation
Overwhelming the opponent with food-countered creatures
Maintaining combo continuity via artifact recursion

keep_criteria: Hands containing Myr Retriever or Cauldron Familiar with two lands
Hands with Lembas and early mana sources
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking combo pieces or early mana
Hands vulnerable to early aggression without protection

early_game: Play lands and cheap combo pieces like Cauldron Familiar
Protect key creatures from early removal
Cast Lembas to establish food counters
mid_game: Use Myr Retriever to fetch artifacts that enable the combo
Move food counters to creatures for stats and life gain
late_game: Execute the food counter loop for infinite life or board control
Close out the game with unblockable threats

key_cards.name: Myr Retriever
key_cards.notes: Use its enter-the-battlefield trigger to fetch key artifacts from the graveyard, enabling combo continuity and protecting against one-time removal.

key_cards.name: Cauldron Familiar
key_cards.notes: Deploy to strip food counters from the opponent's graveyard and place them on your creatures, boosting stats and enabling life gain loops.

key_cards.name: Lembas
key_cards.notes: Cast early to generate initial counters; tap and remove counters to gain life or fuel the combo, ensuring a steady supply of resources.

sequencing_tips: Cast Cauldron Familiar before moving counters to maximize board impact
Prioritize protecting Myr Retriever to maintain artifact recursion
Use Lembas's life gain to stabilize against aggressive matchups

matchups.advice: Prioritize life gain with Lembas and protect combo pieces until the loop resolves.
Play around counterspells and removal by splitting mana and using artifact recursion to recover key pieces.
Race the opponent by assembling the combo quickly while using food counters to outvalue their threats.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and signature cards indicate a food-counter and artifact recursion combo strategy.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Derived from Cauldron Familiar's counter manipulation and Lembas's life gain potential.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Based on standard combo sequencing principles and the provided card interactions.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Pauper format threats that disrupt combo and graveyard-dependent strategies.
